title: Science Basics O/X
description: Everyday facts about the natural world

cards.prompt: Water freezes at 0 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure.
cards.explanation: At standard pressure, water turns to ice at 0 degrees Celsius (32 degrees Fahrenheit).
The Sun is the star at the center of our Solar System, producing light and heat by nuclear fusion.

cards.prompt: Humans breathe in oxygen and breathe out carbon dioxide.
cards.explanation: In respiration, we take in oxygen and release carbon dioxide as a waste product.

cards.prompt: Plants make their own food through photosynthesis.
cards.explanation: Plants use sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide to make food through photosynthesis.

cards.prompt: The chemical formula for water is H2O.
cards.explanation: Each water molecule is made of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom, written as H2O.
Light travels far faster (about 300,000 km/s) than sound (about 340 m/s in air).

cards.prompt: The Earth is the largest planet in the Solar System.
cards.explanation: Jupiter is the largest planet; the Earth is much smaller.

cards.prompt: A healthy human has two hearts.
cards.explanation: A human normally has one heart that pumps blood around the body.

cards.prompt: The Sun revolves around the Earth.
cards.explanation: The Earth and other planets orbit the Sun, not the other way around.

cards.prompt: Bats are a kind of bird.
cards.explanation: Bats can fly, but they are mammals that feed milk to their young, not birds.
Diamond is made of carbon atoms, not gold.

cards.prompt: A year on Earth is exactly 100 days long.
cards.explanation: A year on Earth is about 365 days, the time it takes to orbit the Sun once.
